rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT VS. JDS BUILDERS GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, SEptember 22, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT. Denied. Plaintiff San Francisco Unified School District ("SFUSD") has not established the probable validity of its claim. (CCP sec. 484.090(a)(2).) The only evidence presented in support of SFUSD's motion is the Declaration of Erin Hirst, the Senior Project Manager. Hirst declares that "investigation by expert consultants" revealed that Defendant JDS Builders Group, Inc. ("JDS") failed to comply with the project specifications, which caused SFUSD's damages. (Hirst Decl., pars. 6-7.) The Hirst Declaration is based on hearsay, lacks foundation, and is impermissible expert testimony about the ultimate cause of the SFUSD's damages. Additionally, the Declaration provided to the Court and opposing counsel did not have Exhibits B and C attached. (See Opposition Brief, footnotes 1 and 2.) Therefore, SFUSD has not established "it is more likely than not" that they will obtain a judgment against JDS. (Goldstein v. Barak Const. (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 845, 852.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LEVELLA JOHNSON et al VS. MARYALICE ARRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Levella Johnson'S Attendance And Testimony At Deposition; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 22, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT MARIA GOMEZ, ROCIO GOMEZ, JOEL GOMEZ Motion To Compel Plaintiff Levella Johnson's Attendance And Testimony At Deposition; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED. The Court will sign the proposed order at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID WHITE VS. 540 LEAVENWORTH, LLC, AN CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Of Defendants' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, SEptember 22, 2014, Line 9, Defendants' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatory Nos. 1, 6, and 7 is granted. Defendant did not waive objections. However, Defendant failed in its obligation to provide full and complete responses to a reasonable construction of the interrogatories. (See Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 71, 783.) Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Production of Documents Nos. 2 and 13 is denied. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Production of Documents Nos. 15, 18, 19, 20, 29, 30, 34, 35, and 38 is granted. Within ten days, Defendants are to provide full and complete responses to the interrogatories and document requests listed. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA COLOTTO et al VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, SEptember 22, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANTs SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL and DIGNITY HEALTH's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants' Demurrer is overruled. The Complaint sufficiently alleges a failure of those responsible for attending to the basic needs of the elderly decedent Kenneth Colotto. Further, the allegations in the Complaint amount to recklessness in the commission of such neglect. Defendants' Motion to Strike is denied. First, Plaintiffs have sufficiently alleged a cause of action for dependent neglect. Second, Defendants' reliance on Plaintiffs' failure to comply with CCP section 425.13(a) was explicitly rejected by the California Supreme Court in a case that was cited by Defendants themselves. (See Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 776 ["The Court of Appeal concluded section 425.13(a) does not apply (to punitive damage claims in actions alleging elder abuse subject to heightened civil remedies under the Elder Abuse Act). We agree."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
